tax: Placed at family rank, Static Fulgurants is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a clinging surface-static body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a clinging charged body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a faint glowing mote on surfaces. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a fizzing static residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Static Fulgurants occupies dry charged galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates surface static and recur with the gallery re-charges, sustaining themselves on surface static charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: crawls along conductors and shocks contact. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: clinging mote and surface-charge sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: contact-shock and conductor-crawl. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Static Fulgurants matters because rides gear to shock the hand. It lets survey reports sort clinging charge hazards from striking ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Static Fulgurants is the family-rank grouping of clinging surface-charge elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a clinging static body that crawls and shocks on contact, and divide below into genus groups by charge build and mote size. The crawling contact-shock of dry galleries.
